config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, kekuasaan bisa mudah menyalahgunakan alasan “demi umum.” Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati, bukan dari pengorbanan hak secara membabi buta.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should usually come before society’s interests, because once you let “the common good” override basic rights too easily, abuse becomes very hard to stop. From an American perspective, I’m wary of any argument that gives society a blank check, since our history shows how often that language has been used to justify discrimination, censorship, and government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case; public safety and public health can justify some limits, but only when they are narrow, clearly necessary, and accountable. If society’s interests always take priority, then individual liberty becomes conditional, and I do not think that is a healthy standard.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering sekali dipakai terlalu longgar dan akhirnya yang lemah justru dikorbankan. Memang benar ada situasi tertentu, seperti bencana atau kesehatan publik, di mana pembatasan hak bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sangat terbatas, transparan, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan pembenaran umum untuk menekan kebebasan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap mengurus kepentingan bersama. Kalau hak individu dianggap selalu nomor dua, saya khawatir yang terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa atas nama “masyarakat.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should come first in principle, because in the United States we have seen too many cases where “society” or “the common good” was used to excuse racism, censorship, surveillance, and other abuses. I do think there are real exceptions for public safety and public health, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and temporary, not a default rule that lets the majority steamroll everyone else. When people say society should always take priority, I hear a dangerous blank check for power, and I do not trust that. A healthy society protects individual rights precisely because that is what keeps collective power from turning oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan kelompok yang lemah. Memang ada keadaan luar biasa seperti bencana atau wabah yang menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi itu harus ketat, sementara, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan prinsip umum. Kalau hak individu selalu dianggap nomor dua, yang sering terjadi bukan solidaritas, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa dengan wajah kepentingan bersama. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru yang berani melindungi hak dasar setiap orang sambil mengatur kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse discrimination, censorship, and government overreach. The other side is right that emergencies like pandemics or disasters can justify limited restrictions, but those limits need to be narrow, temporary, and accountable, not a standing principle that lets the majority override everyone else. If individual rights are always treated as secondary, then “the common good” becomes a convenient slogan for abuse. A strong society is one that protects basic rights first, because that is what keeps collective power from turning oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
